content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Ms. Jane Dolan 
Butte county Supervisor 
P.o. Box 3700 
Chico, 
CA 
95927 
Dear Ms. Dolan: 
May 1, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-87-097 
You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure 
provisions of the Political Reform Act.!/ 
FACTS 
You are a County Supervisor in Butte County. 
You are not up 
for re-election in 1987. 
QUESTION 
If you decide to terminate your controlled committee and do 
not raise any additional money, will you lncur reporting 
obligations during non-election years? 
CONCLUSION 
If, as an elected officer, you receive a salary of $100 or 
more per month, you must file semi-annual campaign statements each 
year. 
This is true whether or not you have a controlled committee. 
ANALYSIS 
Elected county officeholders whose salaries are $100 or more 
per month (except judges) must file semi-annual statements for 
each half of the year, whether or not they have received any 
contributions or made any expenditures during the six-month period 
covered by the statement. 
Officeholders whose salaries are less 
than $100 per month and judges must file semi-annual statements 
only if they have received contributions or made expenditures 
during the six-month period covered by the statement. 
(Government 
Code section 84200.) 
!/Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code 
section 18000, et sea. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 o~t~california Administrative Code. 


Jane Dolan 
Page 2 
If you terminate your controlled co~~ittee, you may reduce 
your filing obligations by filing a Form 470, Candidate and 
Officeholder Campaign statement-Short Form (copy enclosed). 
Form 
470 may be used by officeholders who do not have controlled 
committees and who will not receive $500 or more in contributions 
and will not make expenditures of $500 or more during the entire 
calendar year. 
If you file a Form 470 on or before the filing 
deadline for the first report required to be filed, no additional 
campaign statements need be filed for the remainder of the year so 
long as total receipts and total expenditures remain under $500. 
(Government Code section 84206.) 
If your contributions or expenditures are $500 or more during 
the calendar year, you must file a Form 490, Candidate and 
Officeholder Campaign statement-Long Form and Consolidated 
Campaign statement. 
Please refer to page 9 of the 1987 "Information Manual on 
Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act," which 
was sent to you on March 18, 1987, for detailed instructions 
regarding the filing requirements of an elected county 
officeholder. 
I have enclosed Form 415, Statement of Termination, for your 
use should you decide to terminate your controlled committee. 
If you have any additional questions concerning this matter, 
please contact me at (916) 322-5662. 
DMG:MAK:kmt 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
I' 
• 
I 
f 
By: 
Mary Ann Kvasager 
Political Reform Consultant 


\~ 
JANE DOLAN 
-~--------f'f-;;:'* 
--1\\:-~: -'-~:--' 
~,l+---ill. Supervisor, District 2, Butte County 
:\~~ 
~\1. 
Office: County Building - 196 Memorial Way - Chico, California 
Mail: P.O. Box 3700 - Chico, California 95927 - (916 )891-2830 
March 27, 1987 
Mary Ann Kvasager 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Dear Ms. Kvasager: 
Thank you for your response (A-87-068) to my request for 
information. 
As I understand it, an elected official such as myself 
with a controlled committee must file two semi-annual 
statements in 1987, with possible additional requirements 
if I make major contributions or independent expenditures. 
I have a follow-up question. 
If I decided to terminate 
my committee and not raise any additional money, would 
I be required to file any reports in non-election years? 
Sincerely, 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Jane Dolan 
Supervisor, Butte County 
P.O. Box 3700 
Chico, CA 
95927 
Dear Ms. Dolan: 
March 31, 1987 
Re: 
87-097 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on March 31, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days. 
You also should be aware that your 
letter and our response are public records which may be 
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for 
disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Jeanne Pritchard 
Chief 
Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660
